name: Sneutrino chaotic inflation + leptogenesis
summary: The right-handed sneutrino (scalar superpartner of N_R) serves as both the inflaton (driving chaotic inflation via its mass term) and the source of leptogenesis through its decays at the end of inflation. CP-violating decays produce a lepton asymmetry that is converted to baryon asymmetry by sphalerons. Connects inflation, neutrino masses, and BAU through a single field.

addresses_puzzles.note: Right-handed sneutrino with V ~ (1/2) M_R^2 |~N|^2 drives chaotic inflation for M_R ~ 10^13 GeV. Predicts n_s ~ 0.96 and r ~ 0.1; BICEP/Keck has substantially pressed the chaotic-inflation r prediction.
Inflaton (sneutrino) decays after inflation produce a lepton asymmetry; reproduces eta ~ 10^-10 for natural CP phases and M_R ~ 10^13 GeV. Davidson-Ibarra bound is comfortably satisfied.

touches_benchmarks.note: Quadratic-potential chaotic-inflation prediction r ~ 0.13 is in tension with BICEP/Keck r < 0.036. Variants with shallower potentials (V ~ phi^{2/3} or near-plateau) survive.

introduces.note: Builds on seesaw + MSSM. New parameters: lightest right-handed sneutrino mass, CP phases.

history: Murayama, Suzuki, Yanagida, Yokoyama (1993), 'Chaotic inflation and baryogenesis by right-handed sneutrinos', Phys. Rev. Lett. 70, 1912. Variants with deformed potentials (Ellis-Garcia-Nanopoulos-Olive 2017) address the BICEP/Keck pressure.

references.citation: Murayama, Suzuki, Yanagida, Yokoyama, 'Chaotic inflation and baryogenesis by right-handed sneutrinos', Phys. Rev. Lett. 70, 1912 (1993)
